concept: Might you develop a fighting game where characters don't directly attack each other but instead manipulate a shared energy field that occupies the arena between them? Fighters use precise movements to create waves, vortices, and disruptions in this energy medium, with damage occurring when these disturbances contact opponents. The energy field retains all manipulations, gradually becoming more chaotic and dangerous as the match progresses, requiring adaptation to an increasingly complex battlefield. Environmental elements interact uniquely with the energy field, creating strategic opportunities for fighters who can position themselves advantageously. Master-level play involves creating self-sustaining energy patterns that act as autonomous hazards while setting up devastating chain reactions.
